title: Beach content parity phase
summary: Beach is the thinnest live front-half biome. This phase first adds a compact beach parity pack built around upper-beach carriers and wrack-line life, then deepens beach ecosystem notes in a follow-on step.

execution_order.goal: Prepare a beach content parity handoff with science-safe entry candidates.
execution_order.why_now: Beach is the thinnest biome for structured content and needs parity with coastal-scrub.

execution_order.goal: Implement the beach content parity pack.
execution_order.why_now: New beach entries and authored entities make the biome feel denser.

execution_order.goal: Review beach content pack for science accuracy and density.
execution_order.why_now: Science accuracy is a hard gate; new entries must be verified.
Beach notes need to match the teaching depth of inland biomes.
Notes are the main teaching surface; beach has only 6 vs forest's 15.

guardrails: All new entries must be science-accurate and kid-friendly.
New entries should include real Pacific Northwest coastal species or habitat landmarks.
Authored entities must have meaningful positions that teach spatial ecology.
Sketchbook notes should be poetic one-liners, not fact repetitions.
If dune-lupine or beach-strawberry are used in both beach and coastal-scrub, move them into shared-entries instead of duplicating them locally.
Favor upper-beach carriers and wrack-line life over extra shell additions or scrub-heavy shrubs.
Do not touch field-requests, game.ts, or progression logic — leave those for lanes 1 and 4.
